description_short:
Monthly Community Cafe drop-in sessions at
London W5 2RS
It will be held on the first Saturday of every month at 1:30pm-3:30pm.
The entire Autistic community are welcome: Autistic people and Carers.
No official diagnosis is necessary. Just come on in, relax and meet everyone.
Feel free to stop by for a chat and a hot drink with our lovely volunteers.
For more details of all our Community Cafes, please see our website:
https://www.londonautismgroupcharity.org/community-cafes

description_short:
The Community House,
Grays, Essex.
RM17 6DR.
As always, our community cafes are completely free and for all people within the autistic and wider autism community i.e. Autistics of any age, parents, carers, other family members, professionals and any mix of the aforementioned.
The main aim of our community cafes are to provide the community a safe, inclusive and accessible place for people to come together, meet, connect and share.
There will be free tea, coffee, biscuits and other snacks, sensory items, games and more!
There's no need to register, just drop by.
For a full list of our Community Cafes go to our website here: https://www.londonautismgroupcharity.org/community-cafes

description_short:
NAS Merton Group
Contact Address
http://www.nasbranch.org.uk/merton
Description
NAS Merton Group is a locally run National Autistic Society Parents Group. This group was set up in November 2015, and is run by local volunteers. We host a play party for children, and regular coffee mornings for the parents in Merton. We are looking to expand our group, and plan to do more activities where children, adults and families can get involved.

The NAS Newham branch is run exclusively by volunteers who are parents of children on the autism spectrum.
The branch was launched in 2010 and covers the London Borough of Newham.
The branch aims to offer support to parents and carers of children, but also to adults affected by autism.

description_short:
NAS Sutton Branch
Contact Address
http://www.sutton.webeden.co.uk
Description
NAS Sutton is run by parents of young children on the spectrum. They are there to support parents, families, carers and professionals who work with children with autism spectrum disorder (ASD). At the moment, they are a small that can provide information and signposting to both local and national support networks. They run regular coffee mornings with guest speakers, so members can get together and meet others in similar positions. Their branch website contains details of all local organisations who provide support for families and there is an active Facebook Group that gives up to date information on everything that is going on in the local area.

description_short:
email: nasthurrock.group@nas.org.uk
NAS Thurrock Group provides support and information to families and carers of children with autism and campaigns for better local services for those living with autism in Thurrock. The support group organises regular meetings, fun activities, clubs, days out and social events plus raises awareness in Thurrock schools, Health services and workplaces.

description_short:
Our Mission is to support our clients to achieve their potential as individuals and to facilitate their social inclusion through employment.
https://asmentoring.co.uk/
There are a range of services including:
For Individuals:
Pre-employment support,
Workplace support and Coaching,
For Employers:
Recruitment, Consultancy, Management,
For Families, Individuals and Employers:
Various Workshops run throughout the year.
https://asmentoring.co.uk/what-we-do
Locations
The AS Mentoring team are based in 3 areas of the UK: London, Surrey and Birmingham.
We are very aware of the need for specialist support across the UK and further so we do work with clients remotely via Skype, phone-calls, email or a combination of all. If there is a need, staff can travel to other areas of the UK on an ad-hoc basis for Workplace Support and Coaching; this is something we'd be happy to discuss with you individually.

description_short:
Work means far more than money and productivity; it means self-esteem, confidence and social opportunities.
Many people are informally supported into employment by Dimensions as part of their support plans.
https://www.dimensions-uk.org/what-we-do/supported-employment/
Our Supported Employment team provides expert advice and job coaching for people with learning disabilities and autism to gain employment.
https://www.dimensions-uk.org/feature/2015/explosive-dream-job-man-autism/
email: alexandra.seddon@dimensions-uk.org

description_short:
Future Ambitions is an Employment service aimed at supporting young disabled people aged 16 to 25 in Hackney, Islington, Newham and Tower Hamlets into long term sustainable employment.
Working in Partnership
Future Ambitions wants to support organisations to help them engage with more young disabled people, if you would like more information please email future@scope.org.uk
https://www.scope.org.uk/futureambitions
Description:
Future Ambitions is an Employment service aimed at supporting young disabled people aged 16 to 25 in Hackney, Islington, Newham and Tower Hamlets into long term sustainable employment.
Working in Partnership
Future Ambitions wants to support organisations to help them engage with more young disabled people, if you would like more information please email future@scope.org.uk
https://www.scope.org.uk/futureambitions, Following the success of First Impressions, First Experiences, supported by the Credit Suisse EMEA Foundation, Scope has launched Future Ambitions. This new service is tailored to the individual, providing each customer with one to one support from a specialist Employment Advisor to understand their career goals and aspirations and how to achieve them.

description_short:
Neurodivergent individuals can bring unique and valuable skills to the workplace – from the attention to detail and data-driven thinking typical of people with autism, to the creativity and insight associated with people with dyslexia, dyspraxia and ADHD.
Uptimize courses teach you everything you need to know to impress employers. Get hired & advance your career with our online employability training.
Website: http://www.uptimize.com
Description:
Neurodivergent individuals can bring unique and valuable skills to the workplace – from the attention to detail and data-driven thinking typical of people with autism, to the creativity and insight associated with people with dyslexia, dyspraxia and ADHD.
Uptimize courses teach you everything you need to know to impress employers. Get hired & advance your career with our online employability training.
Website: http://www.uptimize.com, Our best-in-class online video training is designed specifically to unlock the potential of the neurodiverse workforce. Our vision is world where neurodiversity is universally championed, and where organizations thrive thanks to their neurodiverse teams. We are excited to be on a journey and to be collaborating with other like-minded organizations, to make that vision a reality.
